area: Taskflow close / commit bundle scope classification
finding: `taskflow close --historical-delivery` can include unrelated untracked backup files from broad task scope in `targetDeliveryFiles`, even after the real delivery has already landed in a valid historical delivery commit. This can pressure operators to stage or commit another agent's scratch/WIP files just to finish closeout.
expectedBehavior: Historical-delivery close should restrict delivery-file requirements to files proven to belong to the historical delivery, or classify unrelated untracked backup/scratch files as advisory residue with an exact remediation. Broad scopes such as `packages/**` should not turn arbitrary `.bak` files into required close-bundle delivery.
evidenceOrRepro: Fixed by classifying scratch/backup files such as `.bak`, `.backup`, `.orig`, and `.tmp` as advisory residue during historical closeback unless they are explicitly declared as exact deliverables. Verification: `node --strip-types packages/cli/src/commands/taskflow/__tests__/commit-bundle-assembly.spec.ts` covers a broad `packages/**` historical delivery with an unrelated untracked `.bak` and proves it is excluded from `targetDeliveryFiles` and staging.
followUp: Keep explicit file deliverables authoritative; only broad-scope historical closeback scratch files are excluded.
